sensor-size-comparison.pixelDensityDescription: Showing common megapixel counts for each sensor. Larger pixels (bigger cells) capture more light.

what-is-crop-factor.question: What is crop factor and why does it matter?
what-is-crop-factor.answer: Crop factor describes the size difference between a camera's sensor and a full-frame (35mm) sensor. It affects the effective focal length, depth of field, and low-light performance. A 1.5x crop factor means the sensor is 1.5 times smaller than full frame.

full-frame-vs-aps-c.question: What are the advantages of full frame vs APS-C sensors?
full-frame-vs-aps-c.answer: Full-frame sensors offer better low-light performance, shallower depth of field, and wider field of view at any given focal length. APS-C sensors are more affordable, lighter, and give extra reach for wildlife and sports photography.

sensor-size-and-depth-of-field.question: How does sensor size affect depth of field?
sensor-size-and-depth-of-field.answer: Larger sensors produce shallower depth of field at equivalent framing. To get the same framing with a smaller sensor, you either use a shorter focal length (deeper DOF) or move farther away (deeper DOF), both reducing background blur.
